events.reason: Story states the routing/projection problem, boundaries, ownership model, compatibility goal, and fourteen concrete acceptance criteria.
Changed resolver, CLI, workspace and review paths are exercised by the focused fresh-checkout lifecycle E2E rather than file classification alone.
Tests enforce catalog authority, mirror consistency, one canonical read authority, lineage headers, no human-owned overwrite, and legacy fallback.
Story explicitly excludes UI and multiple editable SSOT; changed runtime lane is resolver, projections, migration, lifecycle and evidence binding.
Focused unit tests cover missing/conflicting metadata, malformed lineage, unsupported renderer and preflight no-mutation failures; E2E replays lifecycle.
Focused E2E executes feature and governance fresh-checkout lifecycle including resolve, dry-run migration, PR prepare, status and rendered outputs.
CLI resolve/migrate JSON/text and profile/legacy behavior are exercised in focused tests, including two packet layouts and compatibility fallback.
Shared resolver drives lifecycle consumers; fresh-checkout E2E reaches resolve, migration, PR prepare, status and generated surfaces without orphaned state.
Seven files form one routing/projection runtime lane: resolver and its consumers plus direct unit and focused E2E coverage; no unrelated product surface is bundled.
Classifier marked parse failure not_required, but artifact-routing tests reject malformed projection lineage fail-closed; premise omits relevant parsing failure coverage.
Classifier marked schema failure not_required, but resolver tests fail closed for missing/conflicting metadata and unsupported renderer contracts; premise is false.
Agent-review independence and content-scoped freshness integration suites execute lifecycle regression checks, with focused routing tests preserving lineage and ownership.
Focused lifecycle E2E and resolver preflight tests verify state cannot advance through invalid routing/projection writes and legacy fallback remains available.

events.corrected_premise: Parse and malformed routing/projection lineage failures are required failure modes; test/artifact-routing.test.js verifies malformed routing config and malformed/non-canonical projection lineage fail closed before repository writes.
events.reason: The classifier ignored direct artifact-routing regression coverage. The focused suite passed at the current HEAD, so its source and recorded verification evidence replace the false not_required premise.

events.wrong_premise: schema_failure is not_required and has no relevant evidence.
events.corrected_premise: Schema and metadata validation failures are required failure modes; test/artifact-routing.test.js verifies missing or conflicting Story/profile metadata and unsupported renderer contracts fail closed before writes.
events.reason: The classifier omitted direct resolver validation coverage. The focused suite passed at the current HEAD, so its source and recorded verification evidence replace the false not_required premise.

events.corrected_premise: Parse and malformed routing/projection lineage failures are required; artifact-routing regression tests fail closed before writes.

Independent current-head inspection: malformed config is rejected as invalid_config without changing existing bytes (artifact-routing test), malformed/non-canonical lineage is never treated as current, and projection/canonical writes are preflighted before mutation. The focused suite passed 59/59, so the corrected premise and failure handling are substantively supported.

Independent current-head inspection: schema 0.2 validates every selected and unselected profile for complete artifacts and non-empty canonical paths; generated projections require supported renderer id/version, while conflicting metadata is rejected before routing. The focused suite passed 59/59, including unsupported renderer, incomplete profile, missing canonical, and metadata conflict cases; the corrected premise is substantively supported.
Classifier premise is unsound: parse failure is required; malformed routing and lineage inputs are explicitly tested fail-closed before writes.
